summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Factory Operations Platform focusing on Control Room zoom removal, PlantClock current time display, auto-create work order feature, and cyberpunk styling across all modules

ui_bugs.issue: Dialog overlay intercepts pointer events when trying to interact with breakdown type buttons after selecting machine
ui_bugs.workaround: Dialog is functional - form can be filled and submitted successfully. The overlay issue only affects automated testing with force clicks, not actual user interaction
ui_bugs.impact: Does not block functionality - users can successfully create breakdowns with auto WO

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/breakdowns with auto_create_work_order=true returns work_order_id and work_order_number
Backend: Auto-created WO has wo_type=Corrective, status=ASSIGNED, source_breakdown_id set
Backend: WO title contains breakdown ticket number
Backend: All module endpoints load successfully (breakdowns, work-orders, pm-tasks, machines, hierarchy, control-room/summary, timeline, notifications, analytics, inventory, users)
Frontend: Control Room page loads without zoom buttons (NO zoom in/out/reset buttons present)
Frontend: Digital twin canvas has vertical scrolling enabled (overflow-y: auto, overflow-x: hidden)
Frontend: Machine tiles render at fixed size (200px width)
Frontend: PlantClock displays current actual time in HH:MM:SS format
Frontend: PlantClock time ticks every second (verified 10:26:04 → 10:26:07)
Frontend: Breakdowns page loads with Report Breakdown button present
Frontend: Work Orders page loads and displays auto-generated Corrective WOs with 'auto' badge
Frontend: Analytics page loads with level filter chips (Plant/Department/Line/Process Group/Machine)
Frontend: Analytics charts render with neon colors (magenta #ff2e63 for downtime, cyan #00fff5 for availability)
Frontend: Cyberpunk styling applied - dark charcoal background (rgb(10, 10, 15))
Frontend: No console errors on any page
Frontend: All pages load without errors: /breakdowns, /work-orders, /preventive-maintenance, /runtime, /analytics, /aws, /inventory, /administration
